hero.title:
Building an international harmonised
 approach to training green skills
hero.description: This initiative seeks to cultivate an international community of qualified installers to power the energy transition. As a new approach to training, it focuses on
hero.description2: empowerment, energy autonomy and professional integration.

missionCards.title: Global Benchmark
missionCards.description:
This European qualification provides a globally adaptable, high-quality framework for essential
 solar technical skills.

missionCards.title: Empowerment
missionCards.description:
Providing accessible pathways to green careers, fostering social integration and
 economic independence.

missionCards.title: Excellence
missionCards.description: Developing EU-wide harmonised training in partnership with leading organizations to ensure the highest quality training.

lebanonPilot.title: Powering the Future
lebanonPilot.description: With funding provided by Germany's Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy, the training program is launching in Germany to help meet the critical talent demands of the European energy transition.

germanPilot.title: The German Pilot
germanPilot.description: The pilot programs set to occur in Berlin and Bremerhaven (late 2026) have been crafted with local partners to best facilitate the unprecedented regional job growth projected for the solar sector.
germanPilot.quote: 71% of CEOs already cite skills shortages as their most significant business challenge.
germanPilot.quote2: We're dedicated to supporting green innovations of the future and ensuring
germanPilot.quote3: there is a trained workforce to achieve climate goals.

partnersSection.title: Project Facilitators
partnersSection.subtitle: Working together to create the future of green energy training

partners.title: InnoEnergy
partners.description:
Co-developing the curriculum and 
EU-wide accreditation, bringing decades of experience in sustainable education.

partners.title: WERK
partners.description: Local community partner in Bremerhaven, hosting the practical training at their central facility.

partners.title: BMWE
partners.description: Federal department driving the transition to a climate-neutral economy by funding innovative pioneer solutions for societal challenges via the IGP.

partners.title: greentech.training
partners.description: A specialized training initiative bridging the skills gap by recruiting and preparing international talent for the green transition through expert-led and practical education.

partners.title: Projektmarkthalle Stadtteilzentrum Prenzlauerberg Ost
partners.description: A vibrant community hub and 'KulturMarktHalle' that fosters social integration and neighborhood development, serving as the central location for community-embedded project activities in Berlin.

partners.title: ehb elektro GmbH
partners.description: An established Berlin master electrician and solar specialist (Meisterbetrieb) acting as the technical industry partner for practical installation training and quality assurance.
